assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response addresses deprecated API changes for 1.32 upgrade, not 1.30 as specified in the expectation

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions checking admission webhook compatibility and provides commands to check webhook configurations and events

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking system component resource pressure and provides commands to scale components if resource-constrained

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check kube-system pods status and mentions looking for crashlooping system pods

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics' for checking API server metrics and latency

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions checking service mesh compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32 and potential need for control plane upgrades

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check events and mentions looking at application logs for specific error patterns

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response specifically addresses HPA/VPA behavior changes and includes commands to check HPA status and scaling decisions
